gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/248)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/248)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) يَذْكُرُ أَنَّهُ أَتَى رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مَلَكٌ فَقَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يُخَيِّرُكَ أَنْ تَكُونَ عَبْداً رَسُولًا مُتَوَاضِعاً أَوْ مَلِكاً رَسُولًا قَالَ فَنَظَرَ إِلَى جَبْرَئِيلَ وَ أَوْمَأَ بِيَدِهِ أَنْ تَوَاضَعْ فَقَالَ عَبْداً مُتَوَاضِعاً رَسُولًا فَقَالَ الرَّسُولُ مَعَ أَنَّهُ لَا يَنْقُصُكَ مِمَّا عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ شَيْئاً قَالَ وَ مَعَهُ مَفَاتِيحُ خَزَائِنِ الْأَرْضِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Ibn Fazzal, from Al A’ala Bin Razeyn, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,

‘I heard Abu Ja’far<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> mentioning that an Angel came over to Rasool-Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> and he said, ‘Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Gives you<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> choice – you<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> can either become a servant, a humble Rasool<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup>’ or to be an angel and a messenger. So he<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> looked at Jibraeel<sup style="font-weight: normal">as</sup> and gestured with his<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> hand in humbleness, so he<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> said: ‘A servant, a humble Rasool<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup>’. So he (the Angel) said, ‘The Rasool<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup>, along with that there would be no reduction from what is there for you<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> in the Presence of your<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> Lord<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> by antyhing’. He<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘And with him (the Angel) were the keys of the treasures of the earth’.
en.sarwar: 5. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from ibn Faddal from al-‘Ala’ ibn Razin from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “I heard abu Ja’far (a.s.) saying, ‘Once an angel came to the Messenger of Allah and said, ‘‘Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, gives you the choice to be a servant and a humble messenger, or to be an angel and a messenger.” He looked to Jibril who made a gesture with his hand to be humble. He (the Messenger of Allah) said, “I want to be a humble servant and a messenger.” The messenger (the angel) said, “Despite this, there is no reduction in what is with your Lord.” The Imam said, “With him were the keys to the treasures of earth.’””
